abstract.text: The Metal-backed avatar renderer: encodes a complete VRM frame (skinning, morphs, physics, MToon shading, outlines).

inlineContent.text: owns the per-instance render state for one avatar (or one
avatar slot): the pipeline-state handles vended by
the triple-buffered uniform ring, the morph-target compute system, the
spring-bone compute system, and the cached render-item list.
A typical host integration looks like:

code: let renderer = VRMRenderer(device: device)             // construct once
renderer.draw(in: view, commandBuffer: cb, renderPassDescriptor: rpd)

inlineContent.text: Acquires the model’s internal lock and runs the morph-compute pass.
Encodes the opaque, masked, and blended draw lists in render-queue order, then the MToon outline pass.
copies of its per-frame uniforms and
updates never stall on the GPU finishing the previous frame.
Every pipeline state is created via
in the same process after the first one has loaded is
effectively free — the cache returns the same compiled pipelines.
controls how the renderer responds to invalid
alpha-to-coverage when MSAA is active, smoothly fading cutout edges.
so the renderer can be stored in
. Animation updates running concurrently on the same
from a background actor remain safe because the renderer
acquires), so the scene graph stays consistent during

code: // SAFE: animation on a background thread
// SAFE: rendering on the main thread concurrently
renderer.draw(in: view, commandBuffer: cb, renderPassDescriptor: rpd)

Configure the renderer for stylised silhouette rendering: pure-black
body albedo with a single warm directional rim from
eye materials re-routed through emissive so they self-illuminate at
the iris’s own colour. Idempotent — safe to call before or after
. Any subsequent gameplay-style scene reset would need to
undo each step manually (see comments below).

Character root velocity in world space, used by the spring-bone predict
kernel to apply inertial force opposite to character motion. Set this
each frame from the host application’s locomotion code (e.g., the
vector by which the character translated this frame, divided by
deltaTime). Hair and clothing trail behind character movement when
this is non-zero.

Enables an additive directional rim term in the MToon fragment shader.
For every enabled scene light, the shader adds

abstract.code: pow(1 - N·V, additiveDirectionalRimPower) * max(0, N·L) * lightColor * intensity

abstract.text: on top of the lit pass — independently of base albedo. Designed for
pure-black silhouette setups where the standard rim (which multiplies
against base) cannot survive. Default
; legacy gameplay paths
see no behaviour change.
